input.background: A pharmacist reads that a $95 \%$ confidence interval for the average price of a particular prescription drug is $\$ 30.50$ to $\$ 35.50$.
input.question: Asked to explain the meaning of this, the pharmacist says

output.answer: The pharmacist is incorrect. The confidence interval applies to the population mean $\mu$; it does not apply to the distribution of individual observations.

confidence_interval_lower_bound.description: The lower bound of the 95% confidence interval for the average price of the prescription drug.

confidence_interval_upper_bound.description: The upper bound of the 95% confidence interval for the average price of the prescription drug.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level associated with the confidence interval.

average_price.description: The average price of the prescription drug, which is being estimated by the confidence interval.

meta_info.caption: Misinterpreting a confidence interval.
meta_info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\mu$ When $\sigma$ Is Known
